key_insight: REGEN turns the replay buffer from expert RL training into reusable offline data, enabling expert-to-generalist distillation without expensive coupled rollout-and-backprop training.
engineering_relevance: This reduces compute and infrastructure demands for scaling post-training across math, code, and instruction-following tasks while preserving accuracy comparable to multi-teacher on-policy distillation.
method: Recycle teachers’ replay memories instead of training from multiple teacher models
Train the generalist with offline RL to fully decouple sampling from backward updates
Use a replay-recycling pipeline to lower cost while retaining cross-domain generality
evidence_signal: The abstract says REGEN matches MOPD accuracy across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and instruction following, but at substantially lower cost.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the teachers’ replay buffers, so biased or narrow replay data could limit generalization.
paper_title: REGEN: Replay-recycling for Expert-to-Generalist distillation with Offline Reinforcement Learning
